groups.title: General & Startup
groups.description: Language, additional property files and behavior when the application starts.

settings.description: Preferred GUI language. When unset the JVM default locale's language is used.
Comma-separated list of additional locales to add to the displayed language list.
Name of the additional JMeter properties file to load at startup. Clear to disable loading user properties.
Name of the additional system properties file to load at startup. Clear to disable loading system properties.
Comma-separated list of files describing test plan templates and their descriptions, relative to the JMeter root folder.
File that holds a record of element name changes for backward compatibility.
Expand the whole test plan tree when loading a test plan.
Use the fast JMX loading mode when opening test plans.
Skip the confirmation dialogue when deleting tree elements.
Open local documentation in the GUI instead of the online documentation in a browser.
Path to the XSL file used to generate the Schematic View of a test plan. When empty the embedded stylesheet is used.

groups.title: Appearance & Toolbar
groups.description: Look and feel themes, icons, toolbar layout and display scaling.

settings.description: Theme used on first start, before a choice made in Options > Look and Feel is remembered. Per-OS variants such as jmeter.laf.windows are also supported. With breaktest.laf.legacy enabled, a Swing LAF class name or the strings System / CrossPlatform are also accepted.
Show the legacy Swing look and feels (Metal, Nimbus, System, CrossPlatform) in the Look and Feel menu.
Activate a pseudo-HiDPI mode that increases the size of UI elements which are not correctly scaled by the JVM on high resolution screens.
Scale factor applied in pseudo-HiDPI mode. A value of 2.0 is suggested for HiDPI screens.
Classpath resource containing the toolbar icon definitions.
Comma-separated list of buttons shown on the toolbar; use | for a separator. When unset, the list from the toolbar icon definition file is used.
Toolbar icon size. 48x48 is suggested for HiDPI screens.
Classpath resource containing the tree element icon definitions.
Test plan tree icon size. Larger values are useful on HiDPI displays.
Factor applied to the GUI font size by the Options > Zoom In / Zoom Out actions.

groups.title: GUI Behavior
groups.description: Menus, undo history, refresh rate and quick-add hotkeys.

settings.description: Components (GUI class name or static label) that are hidden from the GUI menus.
Show expert-mode components and settings in the GUI.
Number of items kept in the undo history for test plan tree changes. Set to 0 to disable undo/redo; larger values use more memory.
Interval in milliseconds at which GUI listeners process their event queue.
Check periodically for stable BreakTest releases. Manual checks remain available in the Help menu.
Minimum number of hours between automatic update checks.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+0 (Command+0 on Mac).
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+1.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+2.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+3.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+4.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+5.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+6.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+7.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+8.
Component added by the quick-add hotkey Ctrl+9.

groups.title: Logger Panel
groups.description: The log viewer panel at the bottom of the main window.

settings.description: Show the logger panel when the application starts.
Let the logger panel receive log events even while it is closed. Has a small performance impact in GUI mode.
Maximum number of characters kept in the logger panel. 0 means no limit.
Use the syntax-highlighting text area for the console logger panel.

groups.title: Script Editor
groups.description: The syntax-highlighting text areas used for scripts and text content.

settings.description: Wrap lines at word boundaries in script editors.
Enable line wrapping in script editors.
Enable code folding in script editors.
Maximum number of undo operations in script editors. Set to 0 to disable undo.
Font family for script editors, e.g. Hack. Useful for HiDPI screens. When unset the default font is used.
Font size for script editors. Only applied when jsyntaxtextarea.font.family is also set.
Maximum document length (in characters) for which text rendering with kerning is enabled.

groups.title: View Results Tree
groups.description: Limits and renderers of the View Results Tree listener.

settings.description: Maximum size in bytes of a response that can be displayed. Set to 0 to disable the size check and display the whole response.
Maximum line length before an artificial line break is added, to keep the UI responsive on very long lines.
Preferred position for artificial line breaks; breaking happens on a word boundary between this and the maximum line size.
Content size above which the faster simple view mode is used in the text renderer. Set to -1 to switch off.
Maximum number of results kept in the results tree. Set to 0 to store all results, which might consume a lot of memory.
Order of the renderers in View Results Tree. Class names starting with a dot are relative to org.apache.jmeter.visualizers.
Maximum size in bytes of a document that can be parsed by the Tika engine for the Document renderer. Set to 0 to disable the size check.

groups.title: Saving & JMX Backups
groups.description: Automatic backups and saving of .jmx test plan files.

settings.description: Back up the .jmx file to the backup directory every time the test plan is saved.
Directory where JMX backups are created. When unset, backups go to JMETER_HOME/backups. The directory is created if it does not exist.
Maximum age of backup files in hours since save time. 0 keeps backups forever.
Maximum number of backup files preserved. 0 disables deletion by count.
Automatically save (and back up) the .jmx file before running a test plan.

groups.title: AI Auto Scripting
groups.description: BreakTest AI Auto Scripting (Beta): local AI tool integration for repairing and editing test plans.

settings.description: Local AI tool selected by default in the AI Auto Scripting start dialog.
Edit surface selected by default: gui uses live GUI edit tools; non-gui repairs a copied JMX and offers to merge the repaired Thread Group back afterward.
Optional shared working directory for local AI tools. Tool-specific cwd settings take precedence. Defaults to the open test plan directory, then JMETER_HOME.
Show raw prompt and tool text of the local AI CLI instead of concise progress. Only intended for debugging the AI CLI itself.
Deprecated alias for breaktest.ai.show_raw_output.
Absolute path to the BreakTest agent bridge used by local AI tools. Defaults to JMETER_HOME/bin/breaktest-agent-tool, then breaktest-agent-tool on PATH.
Command used to launch local Codex.
Optional model override for codex exec.
Working directory for codex exec. Defaults to breaktest.ai.cwd, then the open test plan directory, then JMETER_HOME.
Sandbox setting used for codex exec. AI Auto Scripting needs local temp/socket access for the running BreakTest GUI bridge.
Approval setting used for codex exec.
Optional full prompt override. Defaults to the built-in BreakTest/JMeter repair prompt.
Command used to launch local opencode.
Optional model override for opencode run, in provider/model format.
Optional opencode agent name.
Working directory for opencode run. Defaults to breaktest.codex.cwd, breaktest.ai.cwd, then the open test plan directory, then JMETER_HOME.
Command used to launch local Claude Code.
Optional model override for Claude Code, for example sonnet, opus, haiku, or a full model ID.
Optional Claude Code agent name.
Optional maximum number of turns for Claude Code print mode.
Working directory for Claude Code. Defaults to breaktest.codex.cwd, breaktest.ai.cwd, then the open test plan directory, then JMETER_HOME.

groups.title: Engine & Threads
groups.description: Test engine behavior: thread scheduling, shutdown and non-GUI runs.

settings.description: Use lightweight Java 21 virtual threads for BreakTest threads instead of platform threads. Lowers memory per thread and improves I/O scalability for high thread counts.
Enable lightweight cloning of test elements to preserve memory. Disable for complex scenarios with many variables.
How often to check for shutdown during ramp-up, in milliseconds.
How often to check for shutdown during timer delays, in milliseconds.
Number of milliseconds to wait for a thread to stop.
Call System.exit(1) when threads fail to stop in non-GUI mode after the test was explicitly requested to stop.
Force System.exit(0) at the end of a non-GUI test even without failures, in case non-daemon threads spawned by the test would keep the JVM alive.
Pause in milliseconds in the daemon thread before reporting that the JVM failed to exit. Values <= 0 disable the daemon thread.
Port on which non-GUI runs listen for a shutdown message. Set to 1000 or less to disable.
Highest port to try when the initial shutdown port is busy. Set lower than or equal to the port property to disable searching.
Regular expression engine used by extractors and assertions: the historical ORO engine or the JDK java.util.regex engine.
Size of the cache of compiled regex Pattern objects for the JDK regex implementation. Set to 0 to disable.
Size of the compiled scripts cache used by JSR-223 elements.
Port for the built-in mirror server. 0 disables it.

groups.title: Timers & Think Time
groups.description: Timer scaling and the Add Think Times action.

settings.description: Factor applied to the pauses computed by the Gaussian, Uniform and Poisson Random Timers.
Implementation of ThinkTimeCreator used by the Add Think Times action.
Timer GUI class added to the test plan by the default think time creator.
Default constant pause of the created timer, in milliseconds.
Default range of the created timer, in milliseconds.

groups.title: Results File
groups.description: Which fields are written to CSV/XML result files and how.

settings.description: Format of result files.
Timestamp format for CSV output: none, ms, or a SimpleDateFormat pattern such as yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SSS.
Field delimiter for CSV files. Use \t for TAB.
Print field names as the first line of CSV files.
Flush after each line written to XML or CSV output. Reduces data loss on a crash at the cost of performance for intensive tests.
Comma-separated list of JMeter variable names whose values are saved in the result files. In XML output the names must be valid XML names.
Which assertion results to save.
Save the assertion failure message. Only affects CSV output.
Save the data type field.
Save the sample label field.
Save the response code field.
Save the response data. Not supported for CSV output.
Save the response data for failed samples.
Save the response message field.
Save the success flag field.
Save the thread name field.
Save the elapsed time field.
Save sub-results.
Save assertion results.
Save the latency field.
Save the connect time field.
Save the sampler data (e.g. request body).
Save the response headers.
Save the request headers.
Save the response encoding field.
Save the received bytes count field.
Save the sent bytes count field.
Save the request URL field.
Save the response file name field.
Save the host name field.
Save the active thread counts field.
Save the sample count field.
Save the idle time field.
Optional XML processing instruction written on line 2 of XML result files.
Prefix used to identify file names that are relative to the current base.

groups.title: Sample Results
groups.description: How sample results record timestamps and encodings.

settings.description: Record the sample start time instead of the end time. Also affects the timestamp stored in result files.
Use System.nanoTime() for timing; otherwise only System.currentTimeMillis() is used.
Interval of the background thread that calculates the nanoTime offset, in milliseconds. Set to 0 or less to disable the background thread.
Default encoding assumed for response data when the server does not specify one.
Disable the sub-result numbering policy introduced in JMeter 5.0. Can be required when using JMeter for functional testing.

groups.title: HTTP Samplers
groups.description: Shared configuration of the HTTP request samplers.

settings.description: Default HTTP sampler implementation. Blank uses HttpClient5 with HTTP/2 preferred. Selecting HTTP/3 as the protocol on a sampler uses the JDK java.net.http client instead (requires Java 26+). Legacy implementation names are accepted when loading old test plans but are executed by the HttpClient5 sampler.
Add a Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded header to POST requests when the header is missing.
Maximum number of redirects to follow.
Maximum depth of nested frames followed when downloading embedded resources.
Do not mark the main sample as failed when downloading an embedded resource fails.
Store an MD5 hash of embedded resource responses instead of the response body.
Maximum response bytes stored per request. 0 stores the whole response.
Size in bytes of the internal buffer used when reading responses.
Maximum number of bytes preallocated for a response based on its Content-Length header.
Keep-alive time in seconds of the thread pool used for parallel embedded resource downloads.
Normalize redirect URLs by removing /../ segments.
Store embedded resource results in a separate container sample.
Use the lightweight header merging optimization when combining Header Managers.

Beta: HTTP/3 sampling is a beta feature. Send requests on HTTP/3 samplers HTTP/3-only, failing when the server does not answer over HTTP/3. When false HTTP/3 is attempted directly with fallback to HTTP/2 or HTTP/1.1 over TCP. HTTP/3 sampling requires Java 26+; older runtimes log a warning once and fall back to the HTTP/2 implementation.

Beta: HTTP/3 sampling is a beta feature. Prefer HTTP/3 for samplers with a blank/default protocol when the runtime supports it (Java 26+). Uses the JDK client with Alt-Svc discovery: the first request to an origin uses HTTP/2 or HTTP/1.1 and later requests upgrade to HTTP/3, like a browser. Samplers using multipart uploads or proxies keep using HttpClient5.
Socket timeout in milliseconds for the AJP sampler implementation. 0 means no timeout.
HTTP version used by the AJP sampler implementation.
Characters per second to emulate a slow HTTP connection. 0 disables the emulation.
Characters per second to emulate a slow HTTPS connection. 0 disables the emulation.
Enable the loopback pseudo-protocol for testing.
Local host address used for multi-homed hosts.
NTLM domain used when the sampler goes through an authenticating proxy. Use command line flags for user name and password.
Reset HTTP state (close opened connections, reset SSL state) when starting a new Thread Group iteration, so each iteration represents a new user.

groups.title: HttpClient5
groups.description: Apache HttpComponents HttpClient 5 transport configuration.

settings.description: Properties file overriding Apache HttpClient parameters, e.g. hc.parameters in the bin directory.
Do not add the default HttpClient5 User-Agent header.
Preemptively send the Authorization header when BASIC auth is used.
Number of retries to attempt for failed requests.
Retry requests that have already been sent, including non-idempotent ones. Usually false, but useful when testing against some load balancers like Amazon ELB.
Idle connection timeout in milliseconds applied when the server does not send Keep-Alive headers. When 0 or less, the idle timeout only applies if the server sends a Keep-Alive header.
Check connections when the elapsed time in milliseconds since last use exceeds this value.
Absolute connection time-to-live in milliseconds. When > 0 a connection is not reused beyond its TTL; negative values disable TTL-based expiry.
I/O reactor thread count of the async HTTP/2 client. BreakTest creates one HTTP/2 client per JMeter thread to preserve virtual-user connection isolation, so keep this small.

groups.title: HTTP Cache Manager
groups.description: Behavior of the HTTP Cache Manager for resources found in cache.

settings.description: Space or comma separated list of HTTP methods that can be cached.
What to return for a resource found in cache: no sample result, a 200 result with an (ex cache) message, or a custom status.
Response message returned in RETURN_200_CACHE mode.
Response code returned in RETURN_CUSTOM_STATUS mode.
Response message returned in RETURN_CUSTOM_STATUS mode.

groups.title: Cookie Manager
groups.description: HTTP Cookie Manager behavior.

settings.description: Delete cookies with null or empty values.
Allow variable expressions in cookie values.
Prefix of the JMeter variables in which cookies are stored when Cookie Manager saves cookies as variables.
Store cookies as JMeter thread variables.
Check received cookies are valid before storing them.

groups.title: Kerberos (SPNEGO)
groups.description: Kerberos authentication used by the HTTP Authorization Manager.

settings.description: Name of the application module used in jaas.conf.
Strip ports from URLs before constructing SPNs for SPNEGO authentication.
Canonicalize the host name when constructing SPNs for SPNEGO authentication.
Delegate credentials to web servers when using SPNEGO authentication.

groups.title: SSL / TLS
groups.description: TLS behavior of the samplers. Keystore and truststore locations belong in the System Properties group.

settings.description: Share one SSL session context between all threads instead of creating one per thread.
Default HTTPS protocol level, e.g. TLS, TLSv1.2 or TLSv1.3. The effective default may vary with the JVM version.
Space-separated list of protocols to enable, e.g. TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3. Only needed when the target server does not support version negotiation.
Comma-separated list of cipher suites to enable for HTTPS connections.
Reuse the cached SSL context between iterations. Deprecated since JMeter 5.0.
First keystore entry index used with keystores containing many entries.
Last keystore entry index used with keystores containing many entries.

groups.title: Response Parsers
groups.description: Parsers used to find embedded resources in HTML, CSS and WML responses.

settings.description: Space-separated list of parser groups. Each parser needs corresponding .types and .className properties.
HTML parser implementation. Lagarto (default) performs best; Jsoup is the most accurate but slower; the Regexp parser may detect references that are commented out.
Space-separated content types handled by the HTML parser.
CSS parser implementation, based on ph-css by default.
Space-separated content types handled by the CSS parser.
Size of the LRU cache storing URLs found in a CSS file to avoid parsing it repeatedly. Set to 0 to disable.
Let the CSS parser ignore all CSS errors.
WML parser implementation.
Space-separated content types handled by the WML parser.

groups.title: Test Script Recorder
groups.description: The HTTP(S) Test Script Recorder. The properties keep the historical proxy prefix.

settings.description: Gap in milliseconds between requests after which the recorder assumes the user clicked a new URL.
Add a numeric suffix to recorded sampler names.
Format string for new sampler names when the naming scheme 'Use format string' is selected.
URL patterns offered by the 'Add suggested Excludes' button. Separate multiple lines with ;
Default content-type include filter.
Default content-type exclude filter.
Headers removed from recorded Header Manager elements. Cookie and Authorization are always removed.
Content types treated as binary; their request payloads are saved to files.
Directory where binary request payloads are saved.
File suffix used for saved binary request payloads.
Disable recorded samples that resulted from redirects when the generating sampler uses auto-redirection.
SSL protocol used by the recorder.

groups.title: Recorder Certificate
groups.description: The certificate the Test Script Recorder uses to intercept HTTPS traffic.

settings.description: Directory containing the recorder keystore. Defaults to the JMeter bin directory.
File name of the recorder keystore.
Type of the recorder keystore.
Password of the recorder keystore.
Password of the private key in the recorder keystore.
Key factory algorithm used for the recorder certificate.
Alias of the keystore entry to use. Define this to use your own keystore.
Validity in days of certificates created by the recorder.
Generate keys dynamically when supported. When false a single key with no certificate is used.

groups.title: Listeners & Live Statistics
groups.description: Summariser output, aggregate report percentiles and backend listeners.

settings.description: Name of the summariser used for non-GUI runs, e.g. summary. When empty, no summariser output is generated.
Interval between summaries, in seconds.
Write summaries to the log file.
Write summaries to standard output.
Ignore Transaction Controller parent sample results in the summary statistics.
First percentile shown in the Aggregate Report and Summary Report listeners.
Second percentile shown in the Aggregate Report and Summary Report listeners.
Third percentile shown in the Aggregate Report and Summary Report listeners.
Size of the sliding window used to compute percentiles in Backend Listeners.
Size of the large sliding window used by backend metrics.
Percentile estimation algorithm used by backend metrics and the dashboard report.
Backend metrics window mode: a fixed-size sliding window or time-boxed windows.
Interval in seconds at which the Graphite Backend Listener sends metrics.
Interval in seconds at which the InfluxDB Backend Listener sends metrics.
InfluxDB connection timeout in milliseconds.
InfluxDB socket timeout in milliseconds.
InfluxDB connection request timeout in milliseconds.
Number of successful samples after which the Mailer Visualizer sends a success message.
Number of failed samples after which the Mailer Visualizer sends a failure message.

groups.title: HTML Dashboard Report
groups.description: APDEX thresholds and report generation. More jmeter.reportgenerator options live in bin/reportgenerator.properties.

settings.description: APDEX satisfaction threshold in milliseconds.
APDEX tolerance threshold in milliseconds.
Regular expression matching the transaction names exported to the dashboard's statistics table.
Timeout in milliseconds for report generation via Tools > Generate HTML report.

groups.title: Functions & Assertions
groups.description: Defaults of built-in functions and assertion diff output.

settings.description: Date format used by __time(YMD).
Time format used by __time(HMS).
Timestamp format used by __time(YMDHMS).
Custom format used by __time(USER1).
Custom format used by __time(USER2).
Delimiter used by the __CSVRead function.
Number of characters shown in the diff sections of an equals assertion failure.
Marker placed before a differing section in equals assertion diffs.
Marker placed after a differing section in equals assertion diffs.
Size of the compiled JMESPath query cache.

groups.title: CSV Data Set
groups.description: CSV Data Set Config element behavior.

settings.description: Value returned by CSV Data Set when the end of file is reached and recycling is off.
Pipe-separated list of file encodings offered in the CSV Data Set GUI.

groups.title: Other Protocols
groups.description: TCP, JDBC, LDAP and JMS sampler configuration.

settings.description: Default TCP client handler implementation class.
Character set used to encode and decode TCP data. Defaults to the platform default charset.
End-of-line byte value used by TCPClientImpl. Values outside -128..127 disable end-of-line detection.
End-of-message byte value used by BinaryTCPClientImpl. Values outside -128..127 disable end-of-message detection.
Length in bytes of the length prefix used by LengthPrefixedBinaryTCPClientImpl.
Text preceding the status number in TCP responses. When defined, the sampler parses a status code from responses.
Text following the status number in TCP responses.
Properties file mapping TCP status codes to messages.
Pipe-separated list of JDBC driver class names offered in the JDBC Connection Configuration GUI, in addition to the built-in ones.
Pipe-separated list of validation queries offered in the JDBC Connection Configuration GUI, in addition to the built-in ones.
Maximum size in bytes of a JDBC result set retained in the sample result.
String used to represent SQL NULL values in JDBC sampler parameters and results.
Maximum number of LDAP results that are sorted before display.
Use java.naming.security properties when the JMS Point-to-Point sampler creates the queue connection. Set to false to stop passing them.

groups.title: Thread Group Validation
groups.description: The Validate action that rapidly checks a Thread Group runs fine.

settings.description: Tree cloner implementation used to create the tree for validation runs. Must extend org.apache.jmeter.engine.TreeCloner.
Number of threads used when validating a Thread Group.
Number of iterations used when validating a Thread Group.
Ignore timers when validating a Thread Group.
Ignore Backend Listeners when validating a Thread Group.
Force Throughput Controllers in percentage mode to run at 100% during validation.

groups.title: Naming Policy
groups.description: Automatic naming of test plan elements.

settings.description: Prefix used when naming elements.
Suffix used when naming elements.
Implementation of the TreeNodeNamingPolicy interface applied by the Apply Naming Policy action.
Prefix prepended by the Include Controller when building the include file name.

groups.title: Classpath & Plugins
groups.description: Where additional plugin and utility classes are loaded from. Changes require a restart.

settings.description: Directories (separated by ;) searched for additional JMeter plugin classes, in addition to lib/ext. Jar files in sub directories are ignored.
Directories searched for utility and dependency classes (platform path separator), in addition to lib. Entries are added to the system class loader and the JMeter internal loader.
Directories (separated by ;) searched for plugin dependency classes; added to the JMeter internal loader only. Prefer this over user.classpath for plugin dependencies.
Only classes whose name contains this string are scanned as JMeter functions.
Classes whose name contains this string are skipped when scanning for JMeter functions.

groups.title: XML & Parser Caches
groups.description: XML and XPath processing.

settings.description: Path to a properties file containing XML namespace mappings in the form prefix=Namespace.
Size of the cache storing compiled XPath2 queries.

groups.title: System Properties
groups.description: JVM system properties loaded from system.properties: SSL keystores, Kerberos files and JVM-level proxy settings. Values are written to bin/system.properties.

settings.description: Path to the keystore holding client certificates used by the samplers.
Password of the client certificate keystore.
Type of the client certificate keystore, e.g. JKS or PKCS12.
Path to the truststore holding trusted certificates.
Password of the truststore.
JSSE debugging switches, e.g. ssl, all, or a combination like sslctx,session,sessioncache.
Kerberos configuration file. Relative names are resolved against the bin folder.
JAAS login configuration file used for Kerberos authentication. Relative names are resolved against the bin folder.
Directory containing the keytool application, when JMeter cannot find it automatically.
Allow restricted headers such as Host and Connection to be passed through to the server.
JVM-level HTTP proxy host used when no proxy is configured in the sampler.
JVM-level HTTP proxy port.
JVM-level HTTPS proxy host.
JVM-level HTTPS proxy port.
Hosts reached directly, bypassing the proxy. Separate entries with |; wildcards like *.example.com are allowed.
